content.problemStatement: Find all articulation points (cut vertices) in an undirected graph whose removal increases the number of connected components.
content.inputFormat: The input consists of two parameters: n (the number of nodes in the graph) and adj (an adjacency list representing the graph).
content.outputFormat: The output is a list of articulation points in the graph. If no articulation points exist, the output is [-1].

solution.approach: Depth-First Search (DFS) with low and tin values to identify articulation points.
